evals.prompt: Frame a paper topic about AI search ads and advertiser strategy
evals.expected_output: An HTML framing card (or markdown if user specifies) containing: working question, research puzzle, primary literature, gap/tension, contribution claim, unit of analysis, scope boundaries, literature snapshot with 3 query scan, and 5 title candidates with one selected.

expectations.text: Dialogue successfully converges to a concrete framing
expectations.evidence: Check that output contains all 8 framing fields

expectations.text: Phase 2 asks 6 framing questions (or smart-skips appropriately)
Phase 3 performs literature scan with 3 queries
Phase 5 produces 5 title candidates and user selects one
expectations.evidence: Verify titles section with selection marked

expectations.text: Output is either HTML or markdown as requested
expectations.evidence: Check file format matches user's 'html' request

evals.prompt: 帮我把'平台算法治理'收敛成一个可写论文题目
evals.expected_output: A Chinese-language framing card with dialogue conducted in Chinese, containing all standard framing fields, literature snapshot from Chinese-language appropriate queries, and title candidates in Chinese academic style.

expectations.evidence: Check that questions and responses are in Chinese

expectations.text: Output file uses <html lang='zh'> or Chinese labels
expectations.evidence: Check HTML lang attribute or markdown Chinese headings

expectations.text: Framing maintains academic rigor despite language
Title candidates are in Chinese academic style
expectations.evidence: Check titles use Chinese academic phrasing

evals.prompt: Turn this fuzzy idea into a researchable title and output html: I'm interested in studying how LLMs could help automate peer review but I'm not sure what specific angle to take
evals.expected_output: HTML framing card starting from fuzzy idea, going through full 6 phases, converging to a specific researchable question and title. Should include literature positioning showing whether the idea is viable.

expectations.text: Handles fuzzy starting point — narrows to specific question
expectations.evidence: Check working question is concrete, not fuzzy

expectations.text: Phase 1 correctly identifies user stage as 'broad topic'
Phase 3 literature scan identifies gap viability
Final title is specific and researchable
HTML output with all required fields
